interfaces.name: DisplayBufferSource
interfaces.signalFlowRole: UI communication point (not in signal path) - provides envelope shape data for visual display

parameterGroups.label: Playback Control
parameterGroups.rationale: Parameters controlling voice allocation, retriggering, and envelope playback behavior

parameters.name: Monophonic
parameters.description: Enables monophonic mode where only one voice is rendered

parameters.name: Retrigger
parameters.description: Restarts the envelope when a new note is triggered in monophonic mode

parameterGroups.label: Envelope Stages
parameterGroups.rationale: Time and level parameters defining the five AHDSR stages, each time parameter modulatable per-voice

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.description: Controls how long the envelope takes to reach full level after a note-on

parameters.name: AttackLevel
parameters.description: The peak level reached at the end of the attack phase

parameters.name: Hold
parameters.description: The time the envelope stays at the attack level before decaying

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: The time the envelope takes to fall from the attack level to the sustain level

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: The level maintained while the note is held

parameters.name: Release
parameters.description: The time the envelope takes to fall to zero after note-off

parameterGroups.label: Curve Shapes
parameterGroups.rationale: Independent curvature controls for each envelope phase transition

parameters.name: AttackCurve
parameters.description: Controls the curvature of the attack phase

parameters.name: DecayCurve
parameters.description: Controls the curvature of the decay phase

parameters.name: ReleaseCurve
parameters.description: Controls the curvature of the release phase

gaps.question: What is the exact stage calculation order in renderNextBlock/calculateBlock? How does FlexAHDSR differ from AHDSR in its per-sample computation? Are the 'draggable curves' computed differently from AHDSR's curve parameters?
gaps.context: The reference page needs to show FlexAHDSR's specific state machine and curve computation, highlighting differences from AHDSR

gaps.question: What do the three Mode values (Trigger=0, Note=1, Loop=2) actually change about envelope behavior? Does Trigger ignore note-off? Does Loop restart the envelope from the attack phase after reaching sustain? What is the default 'Note' mode behavior?
gaps.context: The Mode parameter fundamentally changes envelope behavior and is a key differentiator from AHDSR

gaps.question: How do AttackCurve, DecayCurve, and ReleaseCurve values map to actual curve shapes? Is this the same formula as AHDSR's curves, or a different implementation? Default is 0.5 (vs 0.0 in AHDSR) - does 0.5 mean linear?
gaps.context: Users need to understand the curve controls, especially since defaults differ from AHDSR

gaps.question: FlexAHDSR uses NormalizedPercentage (0-1) for Sustain and AttackLevel, while AHDSR uses Decibel (-100 to 0 dB). Is the FlexAHDSR value used as a linear amplitude directly, or is there any conversion?
gaps.context: This is a key behavioral difference from AHDSR that affects how users set sustain levels

gaps.question: How do Monophonic, Retrigger, and Mode interact? Does Mode=Trigger override Retrigger behavior? What happens with Monophonic=On, Retrigger=Off, Mode=Loop?
gaps.context: Three-way interaction between these parameters needs clear documentation

gaps.question: How does the DisplayBufferSource interface work? Does it provide a ring buffer of the current envelope shape for UI rendering? Is this the mechanism behind the 'draggable curves' mentioned in the description?
gaps.context: Understanding the UI communication helps explain the visual feedback users see

gaps.question: FlexAHDSR's modulation chains lack parameterIndex in the base data (unlike AHDSR where chains explicitly reference parameter indices). Confirm that AttackTimeModulation targets Attack, AttackLevelModulation targets AttackLevel, DecayTimeModulation targets Decay, SustainLevelModulation targets Sustain, ReleaseTimeModulation targets Release. Is there any difference in how these chains are applied compared to AHDSR?
gaps.context: Need to verify the implicit parameter mapping is correct before documenting it

gaps.question: FlexAHDSR time parameters go up to 30000ms (30s) vs AHDSR's 20000ms (20s). Is there any difference in resolution or calculation at these longer times? Does this interact with EcoMode-like optimization (FlexAHDSR has no EcoMode)?
gaps.context: The extended range and lack of EcoMode suggest different performance characteristics

notes: FlexAHDSR is an enhanced version of AHDSR with several key differences: (1) Separate ReleaseCurve parameter (AHDSR shares DecayCurve for both decay and release). (2) Mode parameter with Trigger/Note/Loop options - a significant functional addition. (3) Sustain and AttackLevel use NormalizedPercentage (0-1) instead of Decibel (-100 to 0 dB). (4) Time ranges extend to 30000ms vs 20000ms. (5) Curve defaults are 0.5 vs 0.0 in AHDSR. (6) DisplayBufferSource interface for UI visualization. (7) No EcoMode parameter - possibly always full-rate or uses a different optimization. (8) IMPORTANT: The modulation chains in the base data have no parameterIndex field, unlike AHDSR where each chain explicitly references its target parameter. The chain names clearly correspond to parameters (AttackTimeModulation->Attack, etc.), so these are treated as modMultiply composites with implicit parameter mapping. This mismatch should be verified in C++ exploration.
